title_en: Inside the fantasy world of Donald Trump
title_zh: 唐纳德·特朗普的幻想世界
rubric_en: “Regime Change” is an eye-popping report on “the most powerful man that the planet has ever known”
rubric_zh: 《政权更迭》是一本关于“地球上有史以来最强大的人物”的惊人报告

paragraphs.en: Pundits often say that Trump World resembles “Game of Thrones” because it is a place where horrid people do horrid things in pursuit of power. Fiddlesticks. The main reason why Trump World is like a dragons-and-zombies saga is that its protagonists live in a land of make-believe.
paragraphs.zh: 评论者常说“特朗普世界”很像《权力的游戏》，因为这是个一些人为了权力会做出卑劣行为的地方。啧。特朗普世界之所以像一部“龙与僵尸传奇”，主要原因在于，它的主要人物生活在一个虚构的世界里。

paragraphs.en: Especially President Donald Trump, whose understanding of reality seems to be based on snippets he once heard and wishes were true. “Regime Change”, an account of his second term by two New York Times journalists, Maggie Haberman and Jonathan Swan, brims with examples.
paragraphs.zh: 尤其是总统唐纳德·特朗普，他对现实的理解似乎只基于过去听过的零散说法和他希望是真实的想法。《政权更迭》这本由两位《纽约时报》记者玛吉·哈伯曼（Maggie Haberman）和乔纳森·斯旺（Jonathan Swan）撰写、讲述其第二任期的书中，充满了这样的例子。

paragraphs.en: America was at its richest “from 1870 to 1913”, when it was “a tariff country”, claims Mr Trump. Actually, Americans are six times richer now than they were in 1913, even if you ignore the full benefits of new inventions such as GPS and antibiotics. The president thinks global trade means foreigners screwing Uncle Sam. So when his commerce secretary showed him that Chinese and Indian tariffs on American goods were not very high, he called the numbers “fucking bullshit” and ordered Natalie Harp, a young blonde aide whose job is to print out positive news for him, to Google up “the real numbers”. Ms Harp, “despite her best efforts”, could not find “the numbers that didn’t exist”.
paragraphs.zh: 特朗普先生称，美国在“1870年至1913年”最富有，当时是“一个关税国家”。实际上，即使不计入全球定位系统和抗生素等新发明带来的全部收益，美国人的财富也已是1913年的六倍。总统认为全球贸易意味着外国人在坑害山姆大叔。因此，当他的商务部长向他展示中国和印度对美国产品征收的关税并不高时，他把这些数字叫作“他妈的胡扯”，并命令年轻的金发助理奈特莉·哈普（Natalie Harp）给他上网搜索“真实数字”。哈普女士“尽管尽了最大努力”，也没有找到“那些并不存在的数字”。

paragraphs.en: Much of the federal government continues to operate normally, responding more or less rationally to old-fashioned facts. But in the White House, fantasy reigns. The Philippines has no drug problems because President Rodrigo Duterte kills drug dealers without fussing about “due process”, Mr Trump told his advisers. Actually the Philippines continues to have a huge drugs problem, and Mr Duterte will face trial in The Hague later this year for crimes against humanity.
paragraphs.zh: 美国联邦政府的许多部门仍在照常运作，对老一套事实作出或多或少理性的回应。但在白宫，幻想才是真实。特朗普告诉他的顾问们，菲律宾没有毒品问题，因为总统罗德里戈·杜特尔特（Rodrigo Duterte）会不讲“应有程序”地杀掉贩毒者。事实上，菲律宾仍然面临严重的毒品问题，而杜特尔特今年晚些时候将在海牙因反人类罪受审。

paragraphs.en: Some courtiers are grounded in reality, but they keep quiet about it. Marco Rubio, Mr Trump’s secretary of state, “never lectured the president or flaunted his own knowledge”, Ms Haberman and Mr Swan write. When facts fail to fit the message, they are memory-holed. For example, the message after the murder of Charlie Kirk, a conservative activist, was that America was being overrun by left-wing terrorists. So the Justice Department website quietly took down an official study showing that far-right terrorists in America kill many more people than left-wingers do.
paragraphs.zh: 有些近臣仍然脚踏实地，但他们对此保持沉默。哈伯曼女士和斯旺先生写道，马可·鲁比奥（Marco Rubio）——特朗普先生的国务卿——“从不训斥总统，也从不炫耀自己的知识”。当事实与其叙事不符时，这些事实就会被“归档”起来。比如，在保守派活动家查理·柯克遇刺后，官方信息是美国正在被左翼恐怖分子吞没。于是司法部网站悄悄下架了一份正式研究报告，该报告显示，在美国，极右翼恐怖分子杀害的人数远多于左翼分子。

paragraphs.en: All of Mr Trump’s aides must profess to believe that the election in 2020 was stolen. One candidate was rejected simply for praising Mike Pence, the former vice-president, who refused to pretend his boss had won. This filter lets Mr Trump select staff who are prepared to lie for him—and perhaps break other norms, too.
paragraphs.zh: 特朗普的所有助手都必须表明自己相信2020年选举是被“偷走的”。一位候选人仅仅因为赞扬前副总统迈克·彭斯而被直接排除；而彭斯拒绝假装他的老板赢得了选举。这道筛选门槛，让特朗普得以挑选出那些愿意替他撒谎的人——也许还愿意突破其他规范。

paragraphs.en: Another crucial tool for manipulating reality is to redefine words. The Democratic Party “is not a political party. It is a domestic extremist organisation,” says Stephen Miller, one of Mr Trump’s most extreme advisers. A terrorist is not, as the dictionary says, someone who uses violence for political or ideological ends. The label can be applied to peaceful protesters and even to groups that do not exist, such as the “Cartel de los Soles” in Venezuela. An emergency need not be a sudden crisis but can include the trade deficit, which America has coped with just fine since 1976. An “invasion” can mean drug-smuggling or even foreigners crossing the border to clean hotel rooms.
paragraphs.zh: 操纵现实的另一项关键手段是重新定义词语。特朗普最激进的顾问之一斯蒂芬·米勒说，民主党“不是一个政党，而是一个国内极端组织”。按字典定义，恐怖分子是指“为政治或意识形态目的使用暴力的人”。但这一标签却被用于和平抗议者，甚至用于根本不存在的团体，比如委内瑞拉的“卡特尔·德洛斯·索莱斯”。“紧急情况”并不必然是突发危机，也可以包括贸易逆差，而美国自1976年以来一直都应对得很好。所谓“入侵”可以指毒品走私，甚至指外国人在边境上去清洁酒店房间。

paragraphs.en: It is not nit-picking to complain about Mr Trump’s abuse of language. Laws consist of words. So when the president asserts the right to change their meaning, he is asserting the right to change the law. If protesters are terrorists, then federal agents who shoot them may be justified. If drug-smugglers are invaders, they can be bombed. If the war on Iran is not a war, as Mr Trump has claimed, then he does not need Congress’s permission to wage it. If he can declare “emergencies” at will, he can wield emergency powers whenever he feels like it. America’s courts have struck down some of these outrageous power grabs, but not all.
paragraphs.zh: 抱怨特朗普滥用语言并不是抓小辫子。法律本身就是用文字构成的。因此，当总统声称自己有权改变这些词语的含义时，其实就是在宣称自己有权改变法律。如果示威者是恐怖分子，那么联邦特工开枪射杀他们就可能被视为正当。如果毒品走私者是“入侵者”，他们就可以被轰炸。如果伊朗战争不是战争，正如特朗普所称，那么他就不需要国会批准即可发动战争。如果他可以随意宣布“紧急状态”，那就意味着他可以在想发动权力时就动用紧急权力。美国法院已经推翻了他的一些离谱权力扩张，但并非全部。

paragraphs.en: Ms Haberman and Mr Swan offer little original analysis. Instead, they offer something that will let readers draw their own conclusions: dogged, meticulous reporting that illuminates how exceptional this White House is. Trump-sceptical readers will love it: already “Regime Change” has shot to first place on the New York Times bestseller list and quickly sold out in bookshops and on Amazon in America.
paragraphs.zh: 哈伯曼女士和斯旺先生并没有提供多少原创性分析。相反，他们提供了能够让读者自行得出结论的东西：这种锲而不舍、细致入微的调查报道，照亮了这座白宫为何如此非同寻常。对特朗普持怀疑态度的读者会喜欢这本书：在《政权更替》已在《纽约时报》畅销书榜上冲到第一，并且很快在美国书店和亚马逊卖断货。

paragraphs.en: Some of the book’s details are trivial: Mr Trump thinks he is a better interior decorator than anyone else; an aide saw him supergluing fancy gold decorations to an Oval Office fireplace. At other times, his vanity has global consequences. The head of the CIA told him that a plan for quick, easy regime change in Iran, as presented by Binyamin Netanyahu, Israel’s prime minister, was “farcical”. But Mr Trump had “a good feeling”. What would the head of the CIA know about intelligence?
paragraphs.zh: 书中的一些细节很琐碎：特朗普先生认为自己是比任何人都更出色的室内装潢师；一位助手看到他把华丽的金饰用超级胶水粘在椭圆形办公室壁炉上。另一些时候，他的虚荣心会带来全球性的后果。中情局局长告诉他，以色列总理内塔尼亚胡提出的伊朗“快速、轻松推翻政权”计划是“荒诞可笑”的，但特朗普先生却“有种好感觉”。情报局长又懂什么？

paragraphs.en: Mr Trump has made billions of dollars from being president. Gulf states that need America’s protection just so happen to invest huge amounts of money in his family’s crypto ventures. Countries that wish to avoid punitive tariffs race to greenlight Trump golf courses or hotel projects. “Neither the president nor his family has ever engaged—or will ever engage—in conflicts of interest,” declare his flacks, week after week, with impressively straight faces.
paragraphs.zh: 特朗普先生当总统期间赚了数十亿美元。恰好需要美国保护的海湾国家，正大量投资于他家族的加密资产业务。那些想要避免惩罚性关税的国家也纷纷批准特朗普高尔夫球场或酒店项目。特朗普及其“发言人”每周都坚定不移地宣称，“不论是总统还是他的家人，过去从未卷入，也永远不会卷入利益冲突”。

paragraphs.en: The authors add depth to this familiar tale of shamelessness. Boris Epshteyn, a lawyer who had helped Mr Trump stay out of prison, was accused of demanding fat pay-offs from prospective second-term appointees to whisper a good word in the boss’s ear, and of bad-mouthing those who refused, such as Scott Bessent, now the treasury secretary. The White House counsel urged Mr Trump to cut ties with Mr Epshteyn (who denies the allegations). Mr Trump said no. Another adviser “explained that Trump…valued lawyers he thought would do anything for him” and hated making enemies of those privy to his secrets. He had been burned before.
paragraphs.zh: 这对熟悉的“无耻”故事，作者进一步补充了更深层的细节。律师博里斯·伊普斯坦曾帮助特朗普免于坐牢，他后来被指控向一些可能的二任人事任命对象索要高额“好处费”，以便在老板耳边说一句好话；而对那些拒绝的人（例如现任财政部长斯科特·贝森特）则在背后说坏话。白宫法律顾问曾劝特朗普与伊普斯坦断绝关系（伊普斯坦否认这些指控）。特朗普没有同意。另一位顾问解释说，特朗普“重视那些他认为会为他做任何事的律师”，并且讨厌把掌握他秘密的人推向敌对。因为他以前已经“被坑过”了。

paragraphs.en: One of Mr Trump’s positive qualities is that he often gives interviews. He spent an hour with the authors, despite knowing them as critics. It was on the 17th day of his war with Iran, Brent crude was more than $100 a barrel—and he was keen to show them plans for his White House ballroom. Then he quoted “a historian” who wrote that “Donald Trump is, without question, the most powerful man that the planet has ever known.” Alexander the Great and the Caesars did not have aeroplanes. Hitler, Mao and Stalin did not have Mr Trump’s global reach. The “historian” who wrote this, it turned out, was a golf caddy. ■
paragraphs.zh: 特朗普先生的一个优点是他经常接受采访。尽管知道这些作者是他的批评者，他仍然和他们聊了一个小时。那是在他与伊朗打仗的第17天，布伦特原油价格已经超过每桶100美元——他热衷于向他们展示白宫舞厅的设计方案。后来他引用了一位“历史学家”的话，说“唐纳德·特朗普无疑是地球上有史以来最有权势的人”。亚历山大大帝和凯撒并没有飞机。希特勒、毛泽东和斯大林也没有特朗普那样的全球影响力。结果写这番话的人，竟然是一名高尔夫球童。■

paragraphs.en: For more on the latest books, films, TV shows, albums and controversies, sign up to Plot Twist, our weekly subscriber-only newsletter
paragraphs.zh: 想了解更多最新书籍、电影、电视剧、专辑和争议内容，请订阅《Plot Twist》——我们的每周仅订阅者阅读时事简报
